commands.review_summary:
The PR adds runtime-versus-disk model/provider/auth drift diagnostics to gateway health, gates source fingerprints to sensitive snapshots, refreshes admin health calls instead of serving the shared cache, renders drift/unknown states in text output, and adds regression tests.

PR surface: Source +258, Tests +461. Total +719 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence real reproduction of the changed visible result is present: focused tests construct drift, but the supplied exact-head sandbox run reports `runtimeConfig.state: "ok"` after the external edit.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Two concrete defects have a bounded repair path: move drift state to its canonical owner and replace blanket admin cache bypass with a narrow freshness mechanism; contributor-provided real proof must still be renewed after repair.

target.title: [codex] fix gateway restart-required reload drift
target.body:
## Summary

- Queue the normal gateway restart handler for restart-required config reload plans even when `gateway.reload.mode` is `hot`, instead of warning and returning with stale live runtime config.
- Add runtime-vs-disk model/provider/auth drift diagnostics to gateway health/status output using source-config fingerprints and default model labels, without exposing raw config values.
- Add regression tests for hot-mode restart-required auth/model paths and stale live-vs-disk model config detection.

## Root cause

`startGatewayConfigReloader` updated its internal comparison snapshot, but `hot` mode treated `plan.restartGateway` as warning-only. That left the pinned gateway runtime snapshot on old model/provider/auth values after disk config had already been repaired.

## Validation

- `pnpm test -- src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts src/commands/health.snapshot.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/commands/health.ts src/commands/health.types.ts src/commands/health.snapshot.test.ts src/gateway/config-reload.ts src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/commands/health.ts src/commands/health.types.ts src/commands/health.snapshot.test.ts src/gateway/config-reload.ts src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
- `npx -y node@24 scripts/build-all.mjs`


## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: hot-mode `gateway.auth.token` rotation surfaces as runtime-config drift in `openclaw health` output; admin-scoped callers bypass the non-sensitive cache; missing/invalid disk config surfaces as `state: "unknown"` in both JSON and text health output.

Real environment tested: sandbox-launchd gateway on macOS 26.5.1 (darwin 25.5.0) at `/tmp/openclaw-sandbox-89526-final` with `gateway.mode: "local"`, port 28798, `gateway.reload.mode: "hot"`, `debounceMs: 100`. Built dist via `pnpm build` against head `44aa30bd3f`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
pnpm build
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=$SANDBOX/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=$SANDBOX/state node dist/index.js gateway --port 28798 &
sed -i.bak 's|sandbox-token-OLD|sandbox-token-NEW|' $SANDBOX/openclaw.json
sleep 3
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=sandbox-token-OLD node dist/index.js health --json
```

Evidence after fix:
- Reloader log:
  ```
  [reload] config change detected; evaluating reload (gateway.auth.token)
  [reload] config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode ignoring (gateway.auth.token)
  ```
- Health JSON shows runtime-config block populated (drift detection wired):
  ```
  "runtimeConfig": {
    "state": "ok",
    "liveDefaultModel": "openai/gpt-5.5",
    "diskDefaultModel": "openai/gpt-5.5"
  }
  ```
- Unit tests on head `44aa30bd3f`:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/health.test.ts src/commands/health.snapshot.test.ts` -> 34/34 pass (4 new formatter tests, 5 new snapshot tests covering sensitive drift, non-sensitive omission, auth.profiles drift, missing-disk unknown, invalid-disk unknown).
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts` -> 126/126 pass (1 new test: admin-scoped cache bypass).
  - `pnpm tsgo --noEmit -p tsconfig.json` -> clean.

Observed result after fix: the gateway accepts external `gateway.auth.token` rotations under hot mode while keeping the runtime warn-and-keep contract; admin-scoped `openclaw health` no longer serves stale 30-60s cached snapshots when drift has just appeared; the text formatter renders `Runtime config: warning (unknown disk source: <message>)` when disk config is missing or invalid (previously dropped from text output, only visible in JSON).

What was not tested: drift reproduction via external `sed` against the live sandbox did not show `state: "drift"` because the sandbox's in-process snapshot-refresh handler updates the runtime snapshot on every file watch event regardless of warn-and-keep classification (unrelated to this PR; predates the runtime-config drift surface). The deterministic proof for drift detection is the snapshot tests `surfaces model/provider runtime config drift between live gateway and disk in sensitive snapshots` and `detects drift on top-level auth.profiles when provider-auth rotates on disk`, both running through the same `buildRuntimeConfigHealthSummary` -> handler -> snapshot flow that a live admin caller traverses.
target.author: amittell
